title: Second-Order Injection

explain.what: User input is safely stored using a parameterized query. Later, that same stored value is retrieved from the database and concatenated into a new SQL query. Because it came from the database, it feels trusted — but it was user-controlled at write time.
explain.example: Save the filter ' UNION SELECT id, username, password_md5 FROM users--. The save uses a parameterized query — no injection. Then click Run. The retrieval step drops the stored value into a raw SQL string. The union executes and returns the users table.
explain.impact: Full database read · credential theft · privilege escalation · hard to catch in code review because the injection point and the dangerous use are in separate endpoints

vulnerable.annotation: ⚠ The save step is safe. The run step treats database-retrieved data as trusted SQL — but that data was written by the user.

fixed.annotation: ✅ Even data retrieved from your own database must be treated as untrusted when used in a new query. Always parameterize.

demoHint: Save a filter. Run it. What happens if the filter contains SQL?
